questions.question: Explain the process of cracking a WEP-protected wireless network.
expectedPoints: Capture sufficient IVs through monitoring or packet injection
Use statistical attacks against RC4 weaknesses
Employ tools like Aircrack-ng to recover the key
The short 24-bit IVs eventually repeat enabling cryptanalysis

questions.question: Compare and contrast passive and active wireless attacks, providing two examples of each and explaining their impact on network security.
expectedPoints: Passive attacks: eavesdropping only, no modification (e.g., war driving, packet sniffing)
Active attacks: involve interaction with the network (e.g., deauthentication attacks, evil twin)
Impact of passive attacks: information disclosure, loss of confidentiality
Impact of active attacks: service disruption, man-in-the-middle attacks, data modification
Detection difficulty: passive attacks harder to detect than active attacks
Defensive measures differ for each attack type
